description: Trail geometry generation in the Room Editor should produce strictly orthogonal corridors (axis-aligned segments only, with 90° turns and no curved interpolation). Touch the trail creation path used by RoomEditor::create_trail_from_footer and trail map-manifest normalization/runtime consumers (ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp, ENGINE/runtime/core/manifest/map_manifest_normalizer.cpp, ENGINE/runtime/core/game_runtime_context.cpp). Implementation direction: represent each trail as a centerline polyline constrained to cardinal directions (up/down/left/right), then extrude by trail width to build one clean perimeter polygon with no self-intersections or tangled interior edges. Constraints/cleanup: preserve existing trail serialization keys in trails_data, allow unlimited corner count, and validate output winding/edge ordering so spawn overlap checks and Area::contains_point remain stable.

Fix the crash when changing map background color from the Room Editor Misc Options panel, and make the background color preview update live while dragging/editing the control. Investigate RoomEditor/OtherSettingsAndControls event wiring and map render state propagation (ENGINE/editor/devtools/other_settings_and_controls.cpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp, ENGINE/runtime/utils/utils/area.hpp). Implementation direction: harden the map-color setter path with null/state guards and immediate repaint signaling so every color delta updates the active room background in-frame. Constraints/cleanup: keep current map-manifest field names/default behavior, avoid deferred-only apply behavior, and ensure no duplicate listeners trigger stale state writes.

Correct floor-texture crop/sink clipping so crop alignment is parallel to the world ground plane (screen bottom/X-axis/horizon), not skewed by tilt orientation. Investigate the crop application path in room rendering and asset texture placement utilities (ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/asset_info.cpp, ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/Asset.hpp, ENGINE/runtime/utils/utils/AnchorPointResolver.cpp). Implementation direction: compute crop bounds in a consistent ground-parallel basis before projection, then apply that basis uniformly across tiles so visual cut lines remain horizontal/axis-consistent. Constraints/cleanup: preserve existing crop parameter schema, avoid changing non-floor asset cropping, and remove any per-tile transform branch that introduces rotational drift.

Custom controller authoring cleanup in the Animation Editor: consolidate reusable controller helpers into a single shared module and make generated controllers derive from the same base behavior contract used at runtime. Relevant code paths include controller scaffolding/service and runtime dispatch (ENGINE/editor/devtools/asset_editor/animation_editor_window/CustomControllerService.cpp, ENGINE/runtime/animation/controllers/shared/, and default custom-controller dispatch classes). Implementation direction: define one canonical base interface/class with lifecycle/update hooks plus utility accessors, move helper APIs (including custom_asset_controller helpers) under ENGINE/runtime/animation/controllers/shared, and ensure DefaultCustomController implements and dispatches every documented default hook. Constraints/cleanup: keep backward compatibility for existing metadata controller keys, remove duplicate helper include paths, and update template generation so new controllers are wired without manual edits.

Weighted Random range editor UX pass in Asset Info: improve weighted slider interaction for both fine control and fast large-range movement. Target WeightedIntRange UI plumbing in devtools widgets and the Asset Info panel host (ENGINE/editor/devtools/widgets.hpp, related widgets .cpp implementation, and Asset Info stack panel code that binds weighted random fields). Implementation direction: add velocity-scaled drag deltas (fine near drag origin, accelerated farther away), keep handle/value label feedback synchronized every frame, and add an optional expanded popup editor for precise numeric input + larger drag surface. Constraints/cleanup: preserve existing serialized weighted-range JSON format (runtime/utils/weighted_range), keep keyboard/manual entry semantics consistent with other sliders, and maintain current min/max clamping behavior.

Asset Info Stack anchor depth editing in RoomEditor should use deterministic per-tick world units: mouse-wheel changes must adjust anchor depth_offset by fixed 1-pixel world-depth steps (not percent/fractional increments). Relevant systems: RoomEditor anchor edit input handling/state, Anchor tools panel numeric bindings, and asset metadata persistence (ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_anchor_tools_panel.hpp, ENGINE/runtime/assets/asset/asset_info.cpp). Implementation direction: quantize wheel edits to integer depth units, push updates immediately to UI numeric fields, and write/read the same stabilized depth_offset values in animation payloads. Constraints/cleanup: preserve existing scroll direction/sign semantics, keep non-depth anchor snapping rules unchanged, and prevent cumulative floating-point drift after repeated wheel input.

Dynamic asset efficiency rendering optimization: introduce explicit depth-band behavior so assets between efficiency_depth and cull_depth render but stop advancing animation, and only apply fog interpolation in that middle band. Relevant systems: RoomEditor/runtime dynamic render settings, asset update tick loop, and fog/render pass controls (ENGINE/editor/devtools/camera_ui.hpp, dynamic renderer config in runtime, and asset animation update path). Implementation direction: classify dynamic assets each frame into full-update, paused+fogged, or culled; skip animation frame advancement for paused assets while still drawing current frame; and bypass fog calculations for fully-near assets. Constraints/cleanup: keep cull-depth behavior unchanged, avoid per-frame heap allocations during band classification, replace horizon-thinning behavior with fog attenuation in the same pipeline, and ensure paused assets resume deterministically when returning to active depth.

Dev Mode Room Editor UI relocation: move the 'Near Fog' control from the Dynamic Assets panel into the Camera panel while preserving the same backing runtime value. Relevant files/systems: camera panel widget construction/binding, dynamic-assets panel layout, and persisted room-editor settings (ENGINE/editor/devtools/camera_ui.hpp and corresponding .cpp, plus dynamic-assets panel builders). Implementation direction: rebind Near Fog UI to the existing camera control stack, remove duplicate dynamic-assets control wiring, and keep serialization key/runtime variable unchanged. Constraints/cleanup: preserve default load/save behavior, ensure only one active control mutates the value, and keep spacing/order consistent with current Camera panel controls.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor (non-zero DY handle split): stop elevated-point floor reprojection by separating horizontal and vertical edit handles. Relevant systems: movement point hit-testing/drag routing in RoomEditor, world<->screen transform helpers, and RoomMovementToolsPanel numeric synchronization (ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp/.cpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_movement_tools_panel.cpp). Implementation direction: provide a ground crosshair handle for X/Z edits only (floor projection allowed) and a distinct elevated/Y handle or wheel path for DY-only edits; route drag modes by selected point DY and active handle type. Constraints/cleanup: preserve snap-to-grid behavior for X/Z when snapping is enabled, keep DY==0 interaction unchanged, and guarantee horizontal drags never reset or mutate Y.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor path binding fix: whenever selected animation or selected path changes, bind UI strictly to that animation’s real movement_paths payload (no stale cross-animation cache and no synthetic [0,0,0] rows unless data is truly empty). Relevant systems: RoomEditor movement_edit_ state lifecycle, RoomMovementToolsPanel path list callbacks, and movement payload parse/build helpers used by animation documents (ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp/.cpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_movement_tools_panel.cpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/asset_editor/animation_editor_window/AnimationDocument.cpp, PlaybackSettingsPanel.cpp). Implementation direction: rebuild path options from the current animation payload on selection change, clamp selected_path_index to valid bounds, and refresh active frame/point views from that exact path instance. Constraints/cleanup: eliminate state leakage across animations/paths, keep movement_paths JSON counts synchronized with UI, and show defaults only when authored path arrays are actually empty.

Asset Info Stack > Movement Editor wheel-Y workflow: wheel-based vertical editing must update DY live while leaving X/Z unchanged, and the editor should explicitly switch into an XY-grid overlay mode during vertical adjustment. Relevant systems: RoomEditor movement input handling + grid/overlay rendering, movement point mutation logic, and RoomMovementToolsPanel Y-field synchronization (ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_editor.hpp/.cpp, ENGINE/editor/devtools/room_movement_tools_panel.cpp). Implementation direction: on wheel-edit enter, switch from floor grid to XY overlay (resolution matched to floor grid spacing), apply fixed per-tick Y updates at current X/Z, and remain in XY mode until click-to-exit restores floor view. Constraints/cleanup: with snapping ON, each wheel tick advances to next snapped 3D Y level at fixed X/Z; with snapping OFF, each tick is exactly 1 Y unit; transitions between floor and XY modes must be reversible and visually explicit.
